2606.31473.title: Von Mises Based Uncertainty Quantification for Closely Spaced Automotive Radar Targets
2606.31473.paper_summary: This work investigates uncertainty-aware deep learning approaches for direction of arrival (DOA) estimation in automotive radar, focusing on probabilistic modeling and downstream integration. A circular-statistics-based von Mises (VM) ensemble (ENS) is compared with an evidential deep learning (EDL) framework based on a normal inverse gamma formulation, yielding a Student t predictive distribution in the Euclidean domain. The ENS framework produces angular predictions parameterized by (mu, kappa), enabling interpretable uncertainty aligned with directional geometry. Performance is evaluated under in distribution and multiple out-of-distribution conditions using risk coverage and ROC or AUROC analyses. Results indicate that ENS achieves lower uncertainty under nominal conditions and exhibits stronger sensitivity to severe perturbations, whereas EDL provides smoother uncertainty variation and slightly improved ranking consistency. Importantly, the ENS representation enables direct probabilistic integration into association modules via closed form VM likelihoods, facilitating a unified detection tracking pipeline. These findings highlight a trade-off between geometric consistency and statistical generality in uncertainty-aware DOA estimation.
2606.31473.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作探討了在汽車雷達中針對到達方向（DOA）估計的具不確定性感知的深度學習方法，重點在於概率建模和下游整合。基於圓形統計的馮·米塞斯（VM）集成（ENS）與基於常態反伽瑪公式的證據深度學習（EDL）框架進行比較，產生在歐幾里得域中的Student t預測分佈。ENS框架生成由（mu, kappa）參數化的角度預測，使得不確定性與方向幾何相一致，便於解釋。性能在分佈內和多種分佈外條件下通過風險覆蓋和ROC或AUROC分析進行評估。結果表明，ENS在名義條件下實現了較低的不確定性，並對嚴重擾動表現出更強的敏感性，而EDL則提供了更平滑的不確定性變化和略微改善的排名一致性。重要的是，ENS表示法使得可以通過封閉形式的VM似然直接將概率整合到關聯模組中，促進統一的檢測跟蹤管道。這些發現突顯了在具不確定性感知的DOA估計中幾何一致性和統計一般性之間的權衡。
2606.31473.author: Vinay Kulkarni et.al.
